metadata.title: Why a Deleted Passenger Gene Creates a Target the Driver Gene Never Could
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

color_semantics.CRIMSON: deleted/depleted/below-threshold (MTAP deleted, MTA accumulation, throttled, death)

metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: no full SAM/MTA biochemistry, no PRMT5 substrate biology, no comparison to CDK4/6 inhibitors in this indication, no MTAP-deletion prevalence in specific cancer types beyond the 30% figure, no other 9p21 passenger gene targets
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

viz.note: Chromosome 9p21 locus: show CDKN2A and MTAP as adjacent blocks being deleted together

beats.narration_text: An oncology team sequences a tumor and finds a deletion on chromosome 9p21. They know what that means — the famous tumor suppressor is gone.

viz.note: CDKN2A chip highlighted as driver deletion; label DRIVER and TUMOR SUPPRESSOR

beats.narration_text: The deleted gene is CDKN2A — the p16 tumor suppressor, one of the most important brakes on cell division. This looks like an obvious target.

viz.note: Question card on SLATE panel: CDKN2A is the driver. MTAP is the bystander. Why does the bystander create the better drug target?

beats.narration_text: The intuitive target is whatever CDKN2A loss does. But the therapeutic insight comes from a passenger gene no one was looking for. Why does the bystander create the better target?

viz.note: p16 -> CDK4/6 -> cell division brake; show deletion removes the brake

beats.narration_text: The naive answer: target what CDKN2A does. CDKN2A makes p16, which brakes CDK4 and CDK6 — the kinases that push cells into division.

viz.note: Show many tumor types with CDK4/6 deregulated — not specific to 9p21 deletion. The target is too broad.

beats.narration_text: CDK4 and CDK6 are already major drug targets across many cancers. That selectivity problem is exactly why CDKN2A loss is a hard target.

viz.note: 9p21 locus: two adjacent genes CDKN2A and MTAP both deleted together. Sweep motion left to right.

beats.narration_text: Now look at the passenger. MTAP sits right next to CDKN2A on 9p21. When the deletion sweeps through, MTAP goes with it — no selection for or against.

viz.note: Show MTAP deleted -> MTA builds up -> MTA inhibits PRMT5. CRIMSON for MTA accumulation.

beats.narration_text: MTAP normally clears a metabolite called MTA. In MTAP-deleted cells, MTA accumulates — and MTA partially throttles PRMT5, an arginine methyltransferase.

viz.note: Single bar: PRMT5 activity at 60% (CRIMSON bar). Survival threshold line above it but close. ILLUSTRATIVE number.

beats.narration_text: The result: MTAP-deleted cancer cells run PRMT5 at only 60% capacity. The cancer cell is already pre-throttled by its own metabolite.

viz.note: Two bars: tumor (CRIMSON, 60%) vs normal (TEAL, 100%). Survival threshold line between them. ILLUSTRATIVE number.

beats.narration_text: Normal cells have full MTAP activity. They clear MTA. PRMT5 runs at 100%. The same inhibitor hits both cells — but only one is already close to the edge.

viz.note: Main visual: two bars drop under inhibitor. Tumor crosses survival line (death). Normal stays above (survival). Core card visual.

beats.narration_text: A PRMT5 inhibitor drops both bars. The tumor bar, already at 60%, falls below the survival threshold. The normal cell bar, at 100%, stays above it.

viz.note: Two-by-two grid: MTAP intact + inhibitor = survive; MTAP deleted + no inhibitor = survive; MTAP deleted + inhibitor = DEAD

beats.narration_text: This is synthetic lethality. MTAP deletion alone is survivable. PRMT5 inhibition alone is survivable in normal cells. Together, in deleted cells, it is lethal.

viz.note: Trial enrollment chip: MTAP deletion by sequencing. Drug labels MRTX1719/AMG-193 as PRMT5 inhibitor in trial.

beats.narration_text: PRMT5 inhibitors are now in clinical trials specifically for MTAP-deleted tumors. The sequencing criteria: 9p21 deletion confirmed by genomic testing.

viz.note: Two bars: MTAP-deleted (CRIMSON, 40%) vs MTAP-intact (TEAL, 5%). Label ILLUSTRATIVE. Source: card seed numbers.

beats.narration_text: Illustrative example: a 9p21-deleted tumor enters a PRMT5 inhibitor trial. MTAP-deleted arm: 40% response rate. MTAP-intact control arm: 5% response rate.

viz.note: Endcard: question -> answer. CANCER BIOLOGY kicker. Passenger bystander, precision target.

beats.narration_text: The driver deletion, CDKN2A, offers no selectivity. The passenger deletion, MTAP, creates a pre-throttled PRMT5 margin that only the deleted cell cannot survive losing.

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: Part of the Cancer Biology series.
